caps.proposition_text: The clinician assesses right lateral malleolar fracture.
The clinician recommends a 3-month recovery period.

evidence.span_text: After reviewing the patient's examination and radiographic findings today, I have had a lengthy discussion with him regarding treatment options. Recommendation was made for an open reduction internal fixation of the right ankle. We discussed the risks and benefits of the procedure as well as the postoperative recovery period following surgery. I advised that it will be 3 months before he can resume his exercise routine. In the meantime, I will prescribe meloxicam to reduce swelling. I recommended he ice and elevate the ankle as well. He will be given crutches to assist with ambulation.

evidence.span_text: Skin: Reports right ankle bruising and swelling.
Musculoskeletal: Reports right ankle pain and instability.

X-rays, including AP, lateral, and oblique views of the right ankle, were obtained in the office and reviewed today. These demonstrate a displaced lateral malleolus at the Weber C level. There is no evidence of medial or posterior malleolar fractures.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician observes mild tenderness over the medial deltoid region of the right ankle.

evidence.span_text: MSK: Examination of the right ankle: Exquisite tenderness laterally. Mild tenderness over the medial deltoid region. Swelling on the lateral side of the ankle. No horrible malalignment.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician observes swelling on the lateral side of the right ankle.
